title: atriplex elegans var. fasciculata
intro: Atriplex elegans var. fasciculata, also known as the fascicled saltbush, is a variety of shrub native to Australia that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Atriplex elegans var. fasciculata include its ability to help with wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves or stems, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
